level_signals: 5+ years of experience leading complex technical programs
Title explicitly says 'Senior Technical Program Manager'
Communicates to executive audiences — implies senior standing
Expected to shape foundational security programs at scale

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have 5+ years of experience leading complex technical programs focused on infrastructure security, data privacy, compliance, or enterprise platform capabilities.
Have a proven record of success driving highly cross-functional efforts across engineering, infrastructure, legal, compliance, and product stakeholders.
Are deeply fluent in the intersection of engineering systems, security controls, and regulatory expectations—and are able to bring clarity and structure across all three.
You will need to deeply collaborate on technical architecture and resolve technical problems in partnership with engineering.

Establish scalable frameworks and build programs around critical security initiatives including vulnerability management, evidence collection, incident response coordination, supply chain risk management, and device security.
Track and enforce OpenAI's privacy and security requirements by translating commitments into engineering milestones and driving their full implementation across systems.
Communicate status, risks, tradeoffs, and decisions clearly to technical and executive audiences and drive cross-functional execution with urgency and care.
Thrive in high-trust environments, bring rigor and systems thinking to ambiguity, and operate with a bias for aligned execution over org boundaries.
Are an empathetic communicator and leader—able to influence across technical and non-technical audiences, and build durable trust with partners.

scope.surface: Security, privacy, IT, and compliance programs spanning infrastructure, insider threat, information security, physical security, and information technology
scope.business_model: B2C and B2B AI products (ChatGPT, OpenAI API, enterprise products)
primary_metrics_inferred: Reduction in internal data access exposure
core_responsibilities: Drive execution of critical security and compliance programs such as vulnerability management, merger and acquisition security and integration, infrastructure hardening, and datacenter security management
Partner with IT, Infrastructure, Application, Legal, Privacy, and Security teams to build scalable programs

Establish scalable frameworks and build programs around critical security initiatives including vulnerability management, evidence collection, incident response coordination, supply chain risk management, and device security
Track and enforce OpenAI's privacy and security requirements by translating commitments into engineering milestones
Create and maintain program-level visibility across risk areas, security milestones, and cross-org dependencies
Communicate status, risks, tradeoffs, and decisions clearly to technical and executive audiences

green_flags: Role scoped clearly to a foundational, high-impact security function at a prominent AI company
Explicitly frames role as 'foundational in shaping how we secure OpenAI's systems' — high visibility
Cross-functional breadth (engineering, legal, policy, product) makes for varied and strategic work
EEO and reasonable accommodations commitments stated explicitly
red_flags: No location or remote policy stated — candidates cannot assess geography fit
Visa sponsorship status is not addressed
The 'You might thrive in this role if you' framing makes all requirements ambiguous between hard and soft — all treated as hard per schema rules but this is an inference

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Senior' and 5+ years requirement)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; no direct reports mentioned)
location.work_auth (inferred as 'open' from EEO language; not explicitly stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description in About the Team)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
compensation.equity_mentioned (set to false; no mention in JD)
ambiguities: All requirements appear under a 'You might thrive in this role if you:' heading, which could imply soft/preferred. However, the items are substantive and directly tied to core responsibilities, so all are treated as hard severity per extraction rules. This is flagged as an ambiguity.

Location/remote policy is entirely absent from the JD body. The cities mentioned in the background check section (San Francisco, Los Angeles) are legal compliance boilerplate, not role location indicators.
Visa sponsorship is not addressed anywhere in the JD.
